_comment: V.A.N.E.T — Thread Data File. gen is auto-aggregated by JS.

goss.title: Colonel and the married woman — anyone got details?
goss.preview: Multiple sources saying this one is real. Storehouse involved apparently.
goss.author: anon

posts.text: Surprised there's anyone here who doesn't know who she is.

Fuck me
Is that who I think it is
Who?
How do you not know her
The guard of honour. Blonde one. Raises the flag every morning at the square.
THAT'S her?

You lucky bastard OP
How'd you manage that

Took this this morning at the square
Same girl right?
Definitely her
I walk past her every morning and never thought I'd see something like this
The gap between those two photos is unreal

Now you have
You're welcome

Found the local paper archive
"Graceful guard captures hearts at morning ceremony"
https://county-hillock-gazette/archives/guard-of-honour-Jane-W-1097-05

"Graceful" lol
If only they knew

Same girl from the photos above
Unbelievable
She's got everyone fooled

Took her to dinner first
Proper gentleman and all that
How much did that cost you
Not as much as you'd think
She looks good even just eating dinner

Earlier that evening
She looks good even in shit lighting
Beautiful
Can barely see her but still gorgeous
Shy at first
That didn't last long I'm guessing
Cute
Not long at all
This position
Fuck
Best view
She likes being on top
Lucky bastard
I need to find someone like her
Good luck with that mate

She asked if it was really that good
Let her try
She couldn't handle it
That's actually kind of funny
Adorable
First time?
Obviously
Best angle
Agreed
You're killing us here OP

First round
Used protection this time

"This time"
What about next time
That pause is worrying
You'll see

Lent her to a mate
She said she's fine with it as long as I'm there too

Bullshit
No way that's true

Nah mate filmed it
Video got leaked on one of those membership sites
Probably not public yet
Link?

Here you go
https://ero_tarts/video/serch?cbir_id=4034222%2FUWLx4iQpAlSaWephdiCAeg5049&cbir_page
Holy shit it's real
How many people have seen this
She's finished if this gets out properly
Classic
Can't go wrong with that
She looks good in every position

Break between rounds
She made tea lol

Naked and making tea
That's almost domestic
Weirdly wholesome
"Between rounds" though
Mission accomplished
You're a god OP
Living the dream

She's flexible as hell
Made her do this
Fuck that's impressive

Opens up a lot of possibilities in bed if you know what I mean
Positions you wouldn't think are possible
What positions are we talking
You're making us all jealous here

I did her ass too lol
Tight as hell but she leaked after I came inside
Honestly that was the hottest part
Fuck
How'd you even get her to agree to that

Just gave her the cold shoulder for a bit
Then she said she'd do anything
That's fucked up mate
But it worked

Smell was rough though
But the taboo made it worth it
She shower after?

Of course we did it in the shower
Normal isn't it?
You're insatiable
Fucking hell
What do you reckon this is?
What is this? Camera footage?
How'd you even get this angle
This is genuinely dodgy
You haven't seen dodgy yet lol
That's straight up illegal
Fucked up
But I'm not complaining

Good mates are worth their weight in gold
This is all for my personal collection lol
Hotel owner's in on it?
This is beyond having connections mate
She has no idea does she
Not a clue
She said she'd be fine even if she got pregnant lol
You're a piece of shit mate
That's fucked
She actually said that?
Her words not mine
If she gets pregnant you're done
Both of you are done
Still hot though
Exactly

goss.title: Military birds you'd shag — post yours.
goss.preview: Hillock garrison edition. You know the one at the square.
goss.author: anon

posts.text: Starting us off. That Feline corporal from the 4th. You've all seen her on drills. Don't pretend you haven't noticed.
The Lupus medic at the east barracks clinic. Every single time I go in there for something I forget what I came for.
There's a Perro signals girl in the 2nd who transferred in last spring. Don't know her name. Don't need to.

You lot are sleeping on the guard of honour at the square. Every morning, raising the flag. Blonde. Vouivre.

I've seen blokes stop mid-march.
>>1200004 Which one's that?
>>1200005 Are you blind? She's been here six months. Raises the flag every morning like clockwork. Proper uniform, hair always done. You'd know her if you'd ever looked up from your boots.
Vouivre though. Don't see many of them down here.
Vouivre, Feline, whatever — have you seen her? That's all the argument you need.
Didn't even clock she was Vouivre till someone mentioned it. Just thought she was bloody gorgeous.
Heard she brings breakfast to the branch office lot every morning as well. Bakes it herself apparently.
That's almost too decent. Makes it worse somehow.
Almost.
Right. I see her now. Christ.
Keep it civil lads. Some of us have to work alongside these women.
Bold of you to assume anyone here knows what civil means.

goss.title: Londinium Sarkaz — what's actually going on?
goss.preview: Official line doesn't add up. Anyone with eyes on the ground?
goss.author: IronHeel

posts.text:
The official communiqués are increasingly thin. I've spoken to two men who came through Londinium in the last month and both said the atmosphere is unlike anything from previous postings. Not violence — just something in the air that's changed.

Anyone with more recent intel?
Don't put this in writing. Even here.
This.
That's not reassuring.
It wasn't meant to be.

goss.title: New Lt is some noble's son apparently.
goss.preview: Barely knows one end of a sabre from the other.
goss.author: Furlong-3

posts.text:
Posted to us from Londinium. Family name I won't write here — you'll know it if you know it. The man has clearly never slept rough a day in his life. Boots unmarked. Uniform pressed by someone else. Looked at the mud like it had personally offended him.

We're in safe hands.
Perfectly standard. The Empire runs on this system. Always has.

What's his sabre form like?

Actually never mind. I don't want to know.

I had to explain to him what a field ration was yesterday. He looked at it like I'd handed him a dead animal.

He then asked if there was a kitchen.
There is a kitchen. He is technically not wrong.
We are going to die.

complaints.title: Army rations taste like shite. A formal complaint, in several parts.
complaints.preview: Part one: what was that brown substance last Tuesday.
complaints.author: Tallow-9

posts.text:
I have eaten things in the field that I could not identify with any confidence. Last Tuesday's evening ration contained a brown substance which smelled faintly of soap and tasted of regret. The biscuits are load-bearing structures, not food. The tea ration is hot brown water that has had a teabag waved at it from an adjacent room.

I am not well.

The brown substance is a mystery that predates our posting here. Local legend says it's been the same batch since the last campaign.

I don't investigate these things any more.
The biscuits held up a tent pole for me once. Structurally very sound. Inedible, but sound.

There's a girl who brings proper baked goods to the branch office every morning apparently. Shortbread, pie, the works. Some of us are out here eating mystery paste and she's over there having a tea party.
Don't tell me that. I can't know that right now.

complaints.title: CO's address. Every week. Identical content. 35 minutes minimum.
complaints.preview: I have memorised it. I can recite it backwards. Please help.
complaints.author: Furlong-3

posts.text:
Same speech. Every. Single. Week.

Section one: the Empire's proud tradition. Section two: the honour of the uniform. Section three: something about duty that I'm fairly certain he's lifted from a pamphlet. Section four: repeat section one.

I have started mouthing it along from the back row. Nobody has noticed. This is the only entertainment I have left.
Last week he used the word 'steadfast' seven times. I counted.
Eight. You missed one near the end.
There are two of us counting. This is how low we have sunk.

complaints.title: Taran trouble in the districts again.
complaints.preview: District 17 kicking off. Same story, different week.
complaints.author: Gravel-07

posts.text:
Two of ours got jumped near District 17 last night. Nothing serious but it's the third time this month. Upper command's saying nothing as usual.

This is going to boil over sooner or later.
They've been restless for years. This is nothing new.
Nothing new? It's been escalating for six months. Keep your head down and it'll sort itself out isn't a strategy any more.
Crack down harder. Simple as that. They do this because there are no consequences.

Crack down harder on what exactly? Half of them are just trying to get to work without being harassed. You lot make it worse every time you go through those streets like you own them.

Which, to be clear, you do not.
>>2300005 Here we go.
Victorian soil. Victorian rules. Don't like it, go back to wherever.
They've been here longer than your family's been Victorian, you absolute clot. Read a history book.
This thread's going nowhere useful. Point stands that we're the ones taking the heat while command does nothing.
Agreed. Though maybe ask why they're angry before deciding they're just animals.
Getting philosophical on a Thursday. Lovely.

complaints.title: That woman in C Squadron — watch yourself.
complaints.preview: Not naming names. Just watch yourself around her.
complaints.author: anon

posts.text:
Not naming names because I still have to work here. But there's a Sergeant in C Squadron who has a habit of pulling in lads she takes a liking to. Private meetings. Late shifts.

My mate declined. Got reassigned to the worst patrol route in the district the following week. Make of that what you will.
I know exactly who you mean. Same thing happened to someone I know. Reported it up the chain. Got told there was no evidence and to drop it.
She's useful to command. Good in the field, keeps her unit in line. They're not going to touch her over something like this.
Useful to command. Right. So the rest of us just manage.
Name? At least give a first letter.
Not here. If you're in C Squadron you already know.
The army's always been like this. Men do it, women do it. The rank matters more than anything else. Always has.
That's not the comfort you think it is.

sports.title: Street fights — results from last night.
sports.preview: Rook vs the big Lupus from the 3rd. Pay up lads.
sports.author: Ashford

posts.text:
Right. Results.

Rook vs the Lupus from the 3rd — Rook in the third round. Anyone who had the Lupus, pay up.

The Feline lad from supply vs Denby — called off. Denby's wrist. Don't ask.

Next card's being put together. Suggestions welcome.

I had the Lupus. Of course I did.

How much did Rook take home?
More than you.
Denby's wrist has been dodgy for a month. Should've seen that one coming.
Put Gravel-07 up next. He keeps talking like he's got something to prove.
Name your man.
Oh this is going to be good.

sports.title: Poker game this Saturday — who's in?
sports.preview: Same place as last time. Farthing owes from last month, pay before you sit down.
sports.author: Moor-11

posts.text:
Saturday. Same place as last time. Starts at nine, don't be late.

Buy-in is the same as last month. Farthing — you still owe from April. Pay before you sit down or you're not sitting down.
I'm good for it. I'll have it Saturday.
That's what you said last month.
I'm in. I'm good for it. Saturday.
In. Someone bring something drinkable this time. Last month's gin tasted like turpentine.
In. And Cinder's right about the gin. Whatever that was, it wasn't gin.

It was gin. It was just bad gin. There's a difference.

Saturday. Nine o'clock. Don't be late. Farthing, bring the money.

field.title: Caladon transfer orders — anyone else get one?
field.preview: Notice came through with no explanation. Asking for intel before I pack my kit.
field.author: IronHeel

posts.text:
Transfer notice came out of nowhere. Caladon direction, no details given beyond a grid reference. Anyone been through there recently? What's the situation on the ground?

Last thing I want is to turn up unprepared.

I was through Caladon six weeks ago. Rough terrain, local population not exactly welcoming. Originium dust levels were elevated near the old processing sites. Take your prophylactics and then take some more.
How bad are we talking?
Bad enough that two men in my unit came back with elevated readings and neither of them knew how it happened. Keep your mask on. Don't trust the local water.

field.title: Still using hand-me-down kit from two campaigns ago. Someone explain.
field.preview: These crossbow mechanisms are older than some of the sergeants.
field.author: Tallow-9

posts.text:
The crossbow issued to me has a mechanism I can only describe as 'vintage'. The tension spring has been replaced so many times the housing is basically scar tissue. Quartermaster shrugged when I raised it.

Who do I have to write to?
You write to nobody. You make do. This is the army.
The resupply budget went somewhere. My guess is it went to Londinium and it's not coming back.
If this thing misfires I'm sending the repair bill to HQ personally.
They'll lose the paperwork. They always lose the paperwork.

dark.title: That blonde guard of honour [IMAGES]
dark.preview: You've all seen her at the square. Here's what she looks like off-duty.
dark.author: anon

posts.text:
You've all seen her at the square. Here's what she looks like off-duty.

Got more if there's interest.

dark.title: Taran scum — share your stories. No sympathy required.
dark.preview: District 17 incidents. What they don't put in the reports.
dark.author: anon

posts.text:
The reports get sanitised before they reach HQ. Everyone knows it. This is where the real version goes.

District 17, last week. Post what you know.

dark.title: Brothel guide — County Hillock area. Updated list.
dark.preview: Old list was out of date. Half those addresses are gone.
dark.author: anon

posts.text:
The list circulating is at least two years old. Three of those addresses are gone — one's a chemist now, one's rubble, one got raided.

Updated below. Use your head.

Ashwick Lane, green door, no sign. Ask for Mae. Feline, older, knows what she's about. Clean as far as anyone can tell. Reasonable rates.

District 9, near the tannery. Don't ask names, just knock twice. Mix of girls, Lupus and Perro mostly. Cheaper but you get what you pay for.

Morrow Street — don't bother. Closed three weeks ago after one of the girls tested positive. Nobody wants that kind of trouble.
Can confirm Ashwick Lane. Mae's got a Perro girl working there now as well. Goes by Nell. Worth asking for.
The tannery place — how serious are they about checking? Asking for a mate who's been lazy about his prophylactics.
They're not. Which is why I said you get what you pay for. Your mate's an idiot.
Anyone know if the place near the east gate is still running? Heard it got bought out by someone connected to the garrison and prices went through the roof.
Still running. Prices are worse than before. Officers only now in practice — they won't turn you away but they'll make you feel it. Stick to Ashwick.

life.title: Decent pubs near your barracks? Hillock edition.
life.preview: The Tallow & Flint's gone downhill. Looking for somewhere that still does a proper pint.
life.author: Groat-5

posts.text:
The Tallow & Flint's gone completely downhill since the new landlord took over. Watered-down bitter and the pork scratchings taste like boot leather.

Anyone know somewhere decent still doing proper pints near the east district barracks?
There's a place on the corner near the old market. No sign on the door. Ask for Mick. Doesn't take kindly to uniforms but if you're in civvies he'll sort you out.
You're in County Hillock, mate. Lower your standards accordingly.
The Dray & Cinder on Wick Street. Rough crowd but the landlady doesn't water anything down and she doesn't ask questions. Cash only.
Cheers. That's more like it.

life.title: Pissing rain. Every single day. I've forgotten what the sun looks like.
life.preview: Three weeks straight. My kit is permanently damp. Send help.
life.author: Sleet-2

posts.text: Three weeks. Three weeks without a single dry day. My boots have grown their own ecosystem. My wool coat has never seen sunlight. I have become one with the mud.
Welcome to County Hillock. You'll stop noticing after the first year.
At least you're not in Caladon. Drought and Catastrophe warnings both. We've got it easy.
Mate I would take a Catastrophe over trench foot at this point.

life.title: How many days until payday? I'm completely skint.
life.preview: Somehow spent it all by the 9th. Don't ask.
life.author: Farthing-8

posts.text:
Somehow I'm completely cleaned out by the 9th of the month. Every single month. Don't ask me where it goes, I genuinely don't know.

How many days left?
Fourteen days. Might as well be fourteen years.
Card games and the pub on the 1st. Don't pretend you don't know.
>>400003 The poker game took half. The other half I genuinely cannot account for.
Fourteen days of rations and misery. Same as every month. You never learn and neither do I.

life.title: That book everyone's on about — worth it?
life.preview: Someone left a copy in the mess. Three lads fighting over it now.
life.author: Tallow-9

posts.text: Someone left a copy of that Ivanhoe thing in the mess. Now three blokes are fighting over who reads it next. Is it actually any good or are we all just that bored?
It's decent. Knights and politics and treachery. Reads fast. Better than staring at the ceiling.
We are just that bored.
